metadata.notes: agent meters per-turn (sidecar metering_usage CREDITS + context_usage_percentage) but the daemon observes none: JSONL carries no model/token/cost, and the sidecar's token counts are CREDITS, with input/output_token_count present-but-ZERO â€” no token/cost trace reaches any Source â†’ daemon incapable, route frozen.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready

Kiro CLI 2.5.1 meters per-turn usage at the agent layer, so `agent_supports: yes`. But the metering NEVER reaches any Source the daemon tails as a token/cost field â€” and even the closest available signal (the `<uuid>.json` metering sidecar) carries CREDITS, not tokens, with the token counters present-but-ZERO. With no token/cost trace anywhere the adapter watches, the daemon cannot satisfy the scenario's load-bearing observable (`TotalTokens > 0`, non-decreasing) â†’ `daemon_capability: incapable`, route **frozen**. This is the same yes/incapable shape as codex/claudecode Ă— cloud-background-agent (agent does it, but it leaves no observable trace), NOT the aider Ă— token-accounting case (where the multi-turn accumulator IS observed in the markdown and only the cache distinction is blind).

## What token-accounting asserts

Primitive: *token reporting* â€” the agent's transcript carries per-turn token counts (input / output / cache read/write) in a parser-extractable field; the User-observable signal is `TotalTokens > 0` after turn 1, `TotalTokens` non-decreasing turn-over-turn, and `CacheReadTokens > 0` (fresh input = 0) on a fully-cached turn (scenario 5.1). The three readyâ†’workingâ†’ready cycles are the unremarkable carrier; the token vector is the thing under test.

## agent_supports: yes

Kiro DOES meter per turn. The live 2.5.1 smoke (.build/refresh/kiro-cli-smoke/FINDINGS.md Â§4) confirms the per-turn sidecar `<uuid>.json` carries `metering_usage` (credits â€” e.g. 0.034), `context_usage_percentage` (populated), and `rts_model_state.model_info.model_id`; the TUI prints a per-turn `â–¸ Credits: 0.03 â€˘ Time: 1s` line (Â§6). So at the agent layer kiro reports a per-turn usage quantity that accumulates across turns â€” the agent performs the behavior. (It reports in CREDITS, a provider-billing unit, not raw tokens â€” see incapable below.)

## daemon_capability: incapable

The kiro adapter is `Source: FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/agent.go`). irrlicht observes only what the JSONL parser emits from those `*.jsonl` files. Two independent walls, the first decisive for `incapable`:

  1. **JSONL â€” no token/cost/model trace at all.** The watched `*.jsonl` event vocabulary is `Prompt` / `AssistantMessage` / `ToolResults` / `Clear` (FINDINGS.md Â§4); none carry usage, cost, or model. `parser.go` states this explicitly at line 21 â€” "The JSONL carries no model/token/cost fields; those live in the <uuid>.json metadata sidecar, which this parser does not read" â€” and emits user_message / assistant / turn_done / tool_result events with NO `UsageBreakdown`, `TokenSnapshot`, or `PerTurnContribution`. So `SessionMetrics.TotalTokens` stays 0 across every turn. The scenario's primary assertion (`TotalTokens > 0`, non-decreasing) has no trace in the Source â†’ `incapable` (architecture: no trace), not `bug` (which would need a trace the daemon mis-handles).
  2. **Sidecar â€” CREDITS, not tokens; token counters are ZERO.** Even the closest enhancement â€” a future sidecar `MetricsReader` reading `<uuid>.json` (the documented v1-deferral, parser.go:21 + FINDINGS.md Â§9) â€” would NOT close the gap: `metering_usage` is denominated in CREDITS (a provider-billing unit), and the per-turn `input_token_count` / `output_token_count` are present-but-UNPOPULATED (ZERO) (FINDINGS.md Â§4). So a sidecar reader recovers a credits number and `context_usage_percentage`, but `tokens_nonzero` can never become true from kiro's own data, and `cost_nonzero` would be a creditsâ†’USD inference the adapter does not make. The scenario asserts nonzero TOKEN counts (and the cache-bucket distinction), which kiro's transport never carries â€” the gap is structural even in the best-case sidecar path, which is why this is `incapable` and not a parser `bug`.

No model is observable either (the JSONL has no model field; `model_id` lives only in the unread sidecar), so the recording's observation vector is `model: unknown`, `cost_nonzero: false`, `tokens_nonzero: false`.

## driver_capability: ready

The token-accounting recipe needs only `send` + `wait_turn` + `sleep`, all implemented by `replaydata/agents/kiro-cli/driver-interactive.sh` (its step vocabulary is send / wait_turn / sleep / slash / keys / session / start_session / reset_session / restart / resume / interrupt / sigkill / exit_clean â€” a superset). No gapped primitive is required â†’ `driver_capability: ready`. The driver is NOT the blocker; the observability wall is.

## Routing

yes + incapable + ready â†’ **frozen**: no recording could ever prove `TotalTokens > 0` for kiro, because the token field reaches no Source. The cell documents the freeze and its unblock path; no spec phases are authored beyond the meta + observation vector.

## What would flip the verdict

Two independent things would have to land: (a) kiro-cli would have to populate real token counts (not just credits) in a place the adapter can read â€” either inline in the watched `*.jsonl` (which would make a JSONL parser change sufficient, flipping toward `full`/`bug`), or at minimum non-zero `input/output_token_count` in the sidecar; AND (b) irrlicht would add a sidecar `MetricsReader` for `<uuid>.json` (the documented v1 enhancement). Until BOTH hold, `tokens_nonzero` cannot be satisfied â€” credits alone don't meet the spec.
caveats: kiro DOES meter per-turn at the agent layer (sidecar metering_usage credits + the TUI `â–¸ Credits: 0.03` line), so agent_supports is yes â€” but the unit is CREDITS (provider billing), not raw tokens. Don't read 'kiro has no usage' from the incapable verdict; it has usage, just not as an observable token/cost field.

Even the best-case unblock â€” a future sidecar MetricsReader reading <uuid>.json â€” recovers CREDITS and context_usage_percentage, NOT tokens: input/output_token_count are present-but-ZERO in 2.5.1 (FINDINGS.md Â§4). So tokens_nonzero stays false even with the sidecar; that is why this is incapable (structural, both transports) and not a parser bug.

The cache-bucket sub-assertion (CacheReadTokens > 0; fresh input = 0 on a fully-cached turn) is doubly unobservable â€” neither the JSONL nor the sidecar carries cache buckets at all. It rides on the same no-trace wall as the primary token accumulator.

model is also unobservable from the watched JSONL (model_id lives only in the unread sidecar), so the recording's observation vector is model: unknown â€” distinct from a recordable cell that pins a concrete model id.

sources.note: Line 21 states 'The JSONL carries no model/token/cost fields; those live in the <uuid>.json metadata sidecar, which this parser does not read.' ParseLine emits user_message/assistant/turn_done/tool_result with NO UsageBreakdown / TokenSnapshot / PerTurnContribution â€” SessionMetrics.TotalTokens stays 0. Grounds daemon: incapable (no token trace in the Source).

Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser}: irrlicht watches only the *.jsonl session files, never the *.json sidecar (the sidecar is read solely for cwd via GetCWDFromTranscript). So the token/cost/model fields that live only in the sidecar never reach a tailed Source.

Â§4: watched JSONL vocabulary = Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear, NO model/usage/cost. Sidecar <uuid>.json has per-turn metering_usage (CREDITS), context_usage_percentage (populated), rts_model_state.model_info.model_id â€” but input/output_token_count are 0 (unpopulated). Â§6: TUI prints per-turn `â–¸ Credits: 0.03 â€˘ Time: 1s`. Authoritative live-2.5.1 evidence: agent meters in credits, tokens are zero even in the sidecar.

Interactive driver step vocabulary includes send / wait_turn / sleep (plus slash/keys/session/reset_session/restart/resume/interrupt/sigkill/exit_clean). token-accounting needs only send+wait_turn+sleep â†’ driver_capability: ready; the driver is not the blocker.

Sibling token-accounting precedent for the no-trace â†’ incapable derivation (aider's cache report never reaches .aider.chat.history.md). Differs from kiro: aider's multi-turn accumulator IS observed (sent/received reach the markdown); for kiro NO token field reaches the Source at all, so even the primary accumulator is blind â€” a strictly stronger incapable.

Contrast: opencode reaches daemon: full because its parser HAS a MetricsReader (parseStepFinish reads tokens.input/output + cache into UsageBreakdown). kiro v1 has no such reader and â€” decisively â€” no token data to read even if it did (sidecar carries credits, token counts zero), so kiro cannot reach opencode's full.
